marketState: neutral — exceptional heat driving near-term demand support, offset by bearish storage surplus and strongly negative seasonal pattern
summary: Natural gas (Aug 2026) trades at $3.164, just $0.20 above the $2.96 fair-value midpoint but well within the model band ($2.46–$3.46), with fair value itself rising to $3.17 over 21 days, meaning valuation headwind is minimal and shrinking. Weather is the dominant near-term bullish signal: gas-weighted demand is running 50% above the 5-year normal and has breached the 5-year maximum in both the 1–7-day and 8–16-day windows — a rare double-stacked above-max reading that signals sustained peak cooling load. However, storage sits +151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish injection surprise last week (+6 Bcf vs forecast), and seasonality is definitively bearish: only 19% probability of prices being higher in 4 weeks with a typical 4-week move of −1.8%. Managed money is at the 18th percentile of its 5-year net position range — a deeply crowded short that creates short-squeeze potential if the heat wave extends — while short-term technicals confirm tactical oversold conditions (1h RSI at 27, 4h price below lower Bollinger band). The winter strip (Jan 2027 at $4.344) shows the market already prices in seasonal tightening; the near-term tactical opportunity is weather-driven and time-limited.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality is strongly bearish at the 4-week horizon (19% probability higher, typical −1.8% move, reliability flagged as Low with a 51 pp 10–90% spread), and storage at +151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a recent bearish surprise provides an ongoing fundamental headwind. While managed money's 18th-percentile net short creates contrarian appeal and the winter strip is well-priced (Jan 2027 at $4.344), the current injection season historically erodes front prices; a sustainable long-term long needs either the storage surplus to erode meaningfully or a better entry price that shifts R:R in the trade's favor.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage builds run below 5-year average for three or more consecutive weeks (surplus narrows to <50 Bcf), or the weather demand signal extends well beyond 16 days with continued above-max readings
longTerm.trigger: Two consecutive EIA weekly storage builds that come in below the 5-year seasonal average pace (surplus narrowing), OR front price pulls back to $2.85–$2.95 (lower third of fair-value band) providing a 2:1+ R:R entry toward the $3.46 band top

shortTerm.rationale: Gas-weighted demand is above the 5-year maximum in both near-term windows (1–7 days rangePos 1.98, aboveMax; 8–16 days rangePos 2.16, aboveMax), the strongest weather setup the model can register, and this demand signal is accelerating (nowDemand 190.0, +13.3 in 24h, +50% vs 5-year normal). Short-term technicals are tactically oversold: 1h RSI at 27, 4h below lower Bollinger band, and managed money at the 18th-percentile net short creates fuel for a short-covering move. Current price at $3.164 remains inside the fair-value band ($2.46–$3.46) with fair value rising to $3.05–$3.17 over the next 14–21 days, so the current level is not stretched.
shortTerm.invalidation: Price closes below $3.02 on the daily (signals weather premium is being faded by bears), or either of the 1–7D / 8–16D demand windows drops back within the 5-year range

risks: Weather forecast revision to cooler temperatures would remove the primary bullish catalyst and expose price to the full seasonal/storage headwind
